changeLog.summary: Issue remediation pass: lazy-loaded heavy tabs (EnergyTab/CalculationsTab/MethodologyTab) via next/dynamic in all 3 calculators; extracted LightingCalculator's inlined Equipment/Energy/Retrofit tabs to tabs/lighting/; wrapped Energy + Calculations tabs in React.memo; documented intentional deferral of DHW CalculationsTab split (lazy-loaded so not bundle-critical); added TabSkeleton shared loader; added doc-sync convention to CLAUDE.md.

project.name: dhw-calculator
project.purpose: Web-based engineering tool for sizing and retrofitting equipment in multifamily residential buildings. Multiple per-end-use calculators (DHW, lighting, in-unit HVAC; planned: RTUs, boilers/chillers, etc.) each producing annual kWh / $ / lb CO2 outputs, current-vs-proposed retrofit comparisons, and step-by-step worked-example math.
intendedUsers: energy managers at MF portfolio owners

framework.name: Next.js
framework.lazyLoading: next/dynamic with ssr:false for heavy tabs (EnergyTab/CalculationsTab/MethodologyTab) — defers recharts + long prose off the first-paint critical path

ui.stylingApproach: inline styles + CSS variables in globals.css + Tailwind v4
ui.charts: recharts ^3.8.1 (lazy-loaded — only paid when a chart-using tab is opened)

tooling.nodeVersion: Node 20+ (Next 16 + React 19 requirement, not pinned in engines)

runtimeIntegrations.apiEndpoints: NONE - app is 100% client-side, no backend, no API routes, no fetch calls to external services
runtimeIntegrations.databases: NONE - persistence is browser localStorage only (key: 'dhw-calc.reports.v1' for reports, 'dhw-calculator:inputs:v1' / 'lighting-calculator:inputs:v1' / 'inunit-hvac-calculator:inputs:v1' for live inputs per calculator)
runtimeIntegrations.externalServices: NONE at runtime. Build-time only: npm registry, GitHub Pages, GitHub Actions.

entryPoints.purpose: Calculator picker - tile grid linking to each per-end-use calculator and the cross-domain reports/compare routes
Domestic hot water calculator - 19 system types (central + in-unit), uses SideTabNav with 13 tabs across 4 workflow stages (Inputs / Evaluation / Documentation / Output)
entryPoints.calibrationSource: ASHRAE Apps Ch. 51, ASPE Vol. 2, AHRI 1300/1700, NEEA HPWH, ENERGY STAR MFNC, EPA eGRID 2022

entryPoints.purpose: Lighting end-use calculator - 7 luminaire categories with occupancy/daylight controls, LPD compliance flag, retrofit comparison. Uses TopTabNav (4 tabs). All tabs extracted to tabs/lighting/.
entryPoints.calibrationSource: ASHRAE 90.1 sec 9, ENERGY STAR Lamps/Luminaires, DLC QPL, DOE BA, IES Lighting Handbook, NEEA MF Lighting Field Study

entryPoints.purpose: Per-apartment cooling + heating calculator - 8 archetypes (PTAC/PTHP/mini-split/window AC/CCHP/ducted central). Uses TopTabNav (6 tabs).
entryPoints.calibrationSource: ASHRAE 90.1 sec 6.4, AHRI 210/240, AHRI 310/380, ENERGY STAR Central AC/HP v6.1, NEEP CCHP, MassSave + NYSERDA field studies

entryPoints.purpose: Cross-domain saved reports library - lists scenarios from all domains in one view, allows load/delete/export
Cross-domain compare view - pick 2-4 saved scenarios for side-by-side diff with delta column

appShell.purpose: Sticky 48px brand bar with hamburger that opens CalculatorPicker drawer. Wraps every route via layout.tsx.
Drawer contents - tier-grouped calculator list with live/next/future status badges.

navigationPatterns.purpose: Horizontal scrollable tab nav with active underline. Conditionally renders scroll chevrons + edge fade overlays + auto-scroll-active-tab-into-view when content overflows. Generic <TId extends string>.
Vertical 220px sticky left rail with collapsible workflow groups. Active group auto-expands. Used when tab count outgrows comfortable horizontal display. Generic <TId extends string>.
usedBy: DhwCalculator (13 tabs across 4 groups: Inputs / Evaluation / Documentation / Output)

calculatorShells.purpose: DHW shell — state + routing across grouped tabs. Hosts auto-size apply handlers (with type-guard predicates, no `as` casts), export handlers, share-link handler. Lazy-loads EnergyTab + CalculationsTab + MethodologyTab via next/dynamic.
calculatorShells.topologyBranching: Central systems show {sizing, tech}; in-unit systems show {combi}

calculatorShells.purpose: Lighting shell — slim after the inlined-tabs extraction. All tabs live in tabs/lighting/. Lazy-loads EnergyTab + MethodologyTab via next/dynamic.
In-unit HVAC shell — slim. All tabs extracted to tabs/inunit-hvac/. Lazy-loads EnergyTab + CalculationsTab + MethodologyTab via next/dynamic.

files: CalculationsTab.tsx (memoized, ~1400 lines — intentionally not split; lazy-loaded)
tabs.notes: DHW tabs at tabs/ root (legacy) - other calculators namespace under tabs/<domain>/. EnergyTab + CalculationsTab + MethodologyTab are memoized + lazy-loaded.
All four tabs now extracted as of the 2026-05-12 issue-remediation pass. Previously EnergyTab/EquipmentTab/RetrofitTab were inlined in LightingCalculator.tsx.

hooks.purpose: DHW input state + URL persistence (?s= base64 share link) + localStorage write-through. setInputs merges DEFAULT_INPUTS for backwards compat with older saved reports.
Lighting input state + localStorage write-through. No URL persistence (deferred).
In-unit HVAC input state + localStorage write-through. Adds switchSystemType helper that pre-populates equipment defaults from chosen archetype.

pipelines.purity: deterministic, no I/O, no Date.now, no Math.random
supportingModules: src/lib/engineering/constants.ts (all DHW coefficients with source citations)

pipelines.method: EFLH (Equivalent Full-Load Hours) by climate zone with override option
pipelines.notable: Normalizes HDD/CDD monthly fraction tables to 1.0 so monthly rollup = annual within float tolerance

sharedInfrastructure.purpose: Multi-domain SavedReport library backed by localStorage. v2 schema (domain discriminator + scenarios[] array). v1 records auto-migrate on read.

Per-domain ENGINE_VERSIONS map. Bump per-domain when math could shift a numeric result by >0.5% on default inputs. Saved reports stamp version at save time; stale banner fires when current version mismatches.

Single source of truth for recharts fill/stroke hex literals. Recharts passes colors to SVG attributes which do NOT resolve CSS var() - must hardcode hex. Exports typed maps per consumer (LIGHTING_CATEGORY_COLORS, INUNIT_HVAC_ENDUSE_COLORS, INUNIT_HVAC_ARCHETYPE_COLORS) + neutrals (CHART_GRID, CHART_AXIS).
Shared loader placeholder for lazy-loaded tabs. Renders a shimmer pattern with optional label. Used as the `loading` prop in every `next/dynamic` tab import.
base64url encode/decode for DHW share links (?s= URL param)

retrofitPattern.purpose: Generic two-column current-vs-proposed widget. Generic <I, R>. Each calculator passes its own runCalc, extractMetrics (domain adapter), renderInputs, proposedPreset, onSave.
Renders kWh / therms / $ / lb CO2 deltas + simple payback when capex provided. Therms kept separate from kWh intentionally (avoids fuel-switching conflation).

exports.trigger: Compliance tab buttons via DhwCalculator handlers

typographyHelpers.purpose: Long-form prose primitives for Methodology tabs (large fonts, generous spacing). Exports Prose, H4, Code, Em, Formula, Table, Callout.

Compact worked-example primitives for Calculations tabs (smaller fonts, tighter layout). Exports Step, Prose, Em, Formula, Sub, Result, KVList, Callout. NOT interchangeable with MethodologyHelpers despite name overlap.

uiPrimitives.purpose: Card / CardHeader / CardTitle / CardDescription - borderless by default, bordered when accent or bordered prop set
Field / NumberInput / SelectInput - form field wrappers
Big metric value display (label + value + sub)
Shared loading skeleton for lazy-loaded tabs (next/dynamic loading prop)

dataFlows.name: User input -> live result
sequence: User types/selects in a tab UI control
Tab calls update(key, value) from the hook
Hook updates state + writes to localStorage
Calculator shell's useMemo(runCalc, [inputs]) reruns
New result propagates to all OPEN tabs. Tabs wrapped in React.memo (EnergyTab/CalculationsTab/MethodologyTab) skip re-render when their props haven't changed by reference.

dataFlows.name: Lazy tab load
sequence: User clicks a tab (Energy, Calculations, Methodology)
Calculator shell renders {tab === '...' && <LazyTab .../>}
next/dynamic triggers chunk fetch on first activation
TabSkeleton placeholder renders during fetch (~30-100ms typical)
Chunk arrives -> React swaps in the real tab component -> hydration
Subsequent activations are instant (chunk cached)

dataFlows.name: Save scenario
sequence: User clicks Save in Reports tab OR retrofit Save button
Component calls saveReport(...) [DHW convenience] or saveScenarios(name, domain, scenarios)
storage.ts stamps id + createdAt + updatedAt + engineVersion = ENGINE_VERSIONS[domain]
Persists to localStorage key 'dhw-calc.reports.v1' as a JSON map

dataFlows.name: Load saved report
sequence: Reports tab calls listReports() -> reads localStorage map -> auto-migrates any v1 records via migrateRecord()
User clicks Load on a row
Component extracts primaryScenario(report).inputs and calls setInputs(...)
Hook merges with DEFAULT_INPUTS (backwards-compat for older reports lacking new fields)
useMemo reruns -> live result

dataFlows.name: Stale engine detection
sequence: Reports/Compare tab reads report.engineVersion
Compares against ENGINE_VERSIONS[report.domain]
If mismatch -> renders 'Stale engine' banner
Loading and rerunning the saved inputs produces current-engine outputs (results object is regenerated, not loaded from the report)

dataFlows.name: Export submittal
sequence: User clicks Export PDF/DOCX/Excel/CSV in Compliance tab
Handler in DhwCalculator delegates to exportPDF/exportDOCX/exportExcel/exportCSV in src/lib/export/
Export module accepts (inputs, result) and builds the file in-memory
file-saver triggers browser download

dataFlows.name: URL share link (DHW only)
sequence: Compliance tab Share button calls shareURL() from useDhwInputs
encodeInputs(inputs) -> base64url JSON
Window prompt with URL containing ?s= param
Recipient pasting URL: useDhwInputs hydrates from ?s= via decodeInputs

dataFlows.name: Retrofit comparison
sequence: RetrofitTab mounts RetrofitComparison<I, R>
Both current and proposed inputs run through the same runCalc
extractMetrics adapter converts each result to RetrofitMetrics {kwh, therms, cost, carbon}
SavingsStrip displays delta + simple payback if capex provided
On Save: saveScenarios(name, domain, [{label:'current',...},{label:'proposed',...}])

constraints.rule: All runCalc functions must be deterministic with no external I/O, no Date.now, no Math.random
constraints.enforcedBy: Calibration tests assert determinism on repeated calls

constraints.rule: 354 calibration tests must pass on every commit. A release cannot ship if engine drifts outside published reference bands.
When engine math could shift a result by >0.5% on default inputs, bump ENGINE_VERSIONS[domain]
App must remain a static export with no backend, no API routes, no server-side code paths
constraints.enforcedBy: next.config.ts output:'export' fails build if API routes are added

constraints.rule: tsconfig.json has strict mode enabled - no implicit any, no unchecked indexed access where typed otherwise
Recharts fill/stroke values must be literal hex strings (SVG attrs don't resolve CSS var()). Source all chart colors from src/lib/chart-palette.ts.
Every coefficient in lib/engineering/constants.ts and every calibration assertion must cite its published source in surrounding comments

Methodology helpers and Calculations helpers are NOT interchangeable despite name overlap. Import from src/components/methodology/Helpers for long-form tabs, src/components/calculations/Helpers for worked-example tabs.

Compare tab must read monthly.monthlyAnnualCost / monthlyAnnualEnergy etc. (system-specific actuals), NOT cross-tech what-if fields (annualGasTherms / annualResistanceKWh / annualHPWHKWh_total) which produce identical values across systems
useDhwInputs.setInputs (and equivalents) must merge with DEFAULT_INPUTS so older saved reports lacking newly-added fields hydrate cleanly
constraints.enforcedBy: Convention - tested implicitly via load-an-old-report flow

constraints.rule: EnergyTab (recharts dependency), CalculationsTab (large), MethodologyTab (long-form prose) MUST be lazy-loaded via next/dynamic in calculator shells with TabSkeleton as the loading placeholder.
constraints.enforcedBy: Convention - no automated check; bundle analysis catches drift

constraints.rule: EnergyTab + CalculationsTab + MethodologyTab MUST be wrapped in React.memo so they skip re-renders on unrelated parent state updates.

README.md, architecture.json, architecture-map.html, CLAUDE.md, and docs/calibration.md MUST be updated in the same commit that introduces architectural changes (new routes, new calculators, schema changes, engine version bumps, nav pattern changes).
constraints.enforcedBy: Convention - documented in CLAUDE.md 'Keeping the docs current' section; relies on reviewer attention + Claude/developer discipline

architectureDecisions.title: Static SPA, no backend
architectureDecisions.decision: App is fully client-side, deployed as static HTML/JS to GitHub Pages. No backend, no database, no API routes.
architectureDecisions.rationale: Engineering calculations are deterministic given inputs - no need for server state. Eliminates backend liability (no auth, no DB ops, no API endpoint surface), simplifies deployment (push -> GH Pages), enables offline-after-load usage.
architectureDecisions.tradeoff: No cross-device sync, no centralized usage analytics, saved reports live only in one browser's localStorage. Mitigated by export/import buttons + URL share links (DHW).

architectureDecisions.title: Pure functional pipelines
architectureDecisions.decision: Each calculator's runCalc is a pure deterministic function with zero external dependencies.
architectureDecisions.rationale: Makes the math trivially testable (no mocking), makes calibration assertions sound (input X always produces output Y), keeps the React layer thin (useMemo + dependencies, no effect chains).
architectureDecisions.tradeoff: All sizing options must be enumerated upfront in inputs (no progressive disclosure via async fetches).

architectureDecisions.title: Per-domain calculators with standardized anatomy
architectureDecisions.decision: Each end use gets its own folder structure (lib/<domain>/inputs.ts + types.ts + pipeline.ts + calibration.test.ts; components/<X>Calculator.tsx + tabs/<domain>/*.tsx; hooks/use<X>Inputs.ts; app/<domain>/page.tsx)
architectureDecisions.rationale: Adding a new end use becomes a recipe rather than ad-hoc work. New calculator authors copy the pattern, swap the engine, ship.
architectureDecisions.tradeoff: Standardization tax - small repetition across calculators (extractMetrics adapters, useState patterns) intentionally kept per-domain rather than over-abstracted.

architectureDecisions.title: Multi-domain SavedReport schema (v2)
architectureDecisions.decision: Saved reports have a domain discriminator + scenarios[] array. One library holds all domains. Single-snapshot reports have 1 scenario; retrofit comparisons have 2.
architectureDecisions.rationale: Cross-domain Reports + Compare features work without per-domain storage. Retrofit comparisons fit naturally as multi-scenario records.
architectureDecisions.migration: v1 records (single inputs/results, no domain) auto-upgrade on read via migrateRecord() - non-destructive.

architectureDecisions.title: Per-domain engine versioning
architectureDecisions.decision: ENGINE_VERSIONS is a per-domain map, not a single global version.
architectureDecisions.rationale: Changes to lighting math shouldn't trigger stale banners on DHW reports. Each domain evolves independently.
architectureDecisions.tradeoff: Authors must remember to bump the right version when changing math.

architectureDecisions.title: Calibration tests pin to published reference bands
architectureDecisions.decision: Calibration tests assert ranges (e.g. 35-65% retrofit kWh reduction) rather than exact values, with comments citing the published source (ASHRAE/AHRI/NEEP/EPA/DOE).
architectureDecisions.rationale: The references publish ranges (field data has variance). Asserting point values would fail spuriously; asserting ranges catches real drift while accommodating intentional refinements within the band.
architectureDecisions.tradeoff: If math drifts within the band, calibration won't catch it. Use specific worked-example tests for that.

architectureDecisions.title: Two navigation patterns (TopTabNav + SideTabNav)
architectureDecisions.decision: Lighting (4 tabs) and in-unit HVAC (6 tabs) use TopTabNav; DHW (13 tabs) uses SideTabNav with collapsible workflow groups.
architectureDecisions.rationale: DHW outgrew comfortable horizontal display. Vertical tree surfaces workflow stages (Inputs -> Evaluation -> Documentation -> Output) explicitly. Other calculators don't need the chrome.
architectureDecisions.tradeoff: Inconsistency across calculators - mitigated by each calculator getting nav appropriate to its complexity.

architectureDecisions.title: Chart palette centralized in JS (not CSS)
architectureDecisions.decision: Chart fill/stroke hex literals live in src/lib/chart-palette.ts, NOT as CSS variables.
architectureDecisions.rationale: Recharts passes colors to SVG attributes which don't resolve CSS var() references.
architectureDecisions.tradeoff: Palette duplicated between JS (chart-palette.ts) and CSS (globals.css). Must update both when adjusting brand colors.

architectureDecisions.title: Inline styles + CSS variables for non-chart UI
architectureDecisions.decision: Components use style={{}} objects with var(--*) references rather than CSS classes or styled-components.
architectureDecisions.rationale: Avoids build-time styling complexity for a small project. CSS variables in globals.css provide a centralized palette. Tailwind is available but used sparingly.
architectureDecisions.tradeoff: Repeated style objects across components. Theme changes require touching many files.

architectureDecisions.title: Generic RetrofitComparison widget
architectureDecisions.decision: RetrofitComparison<I, R> is generic over the domain's input/result types. Each calculator supplies its own runCalc, extractMetrics, renderInputs, proposedPreset.
architectureDecisions.rationale: Adding retrofit comparison to a new calculator costs one component + one 5-line extractMetrics adapter, not a rewrite of the comparison flow.

architectureDecisions.title: Lazy-load heavy tabs via next/dynamic
architectureDecisions.decision: EnergyTab (recharts), CalculationsTab (large), MethodologyTab (long-form prose) are imported via next/dynamic with ssr:false in each calculator shell. TabSkeleton renders during chunk load.
architectureDecisions.rationale: Recharts is ~80-100KB gzipped. Methodology + Calculations tabs are 800-1400 lines each. Eager imports paid all this on first paint of every calculator route. Lazy-loading defers until the user actually visits the relevant tab.
architectureDecisions.tradeoff: Small skeleton flash on first tab visit (~30-100ms). Acceptable trade for the bundle-size win on initial load. Subsequent visits cached.

architectureDecisions.title: React.memo on stable heavy tabs
architectureDecisions.decision: EnergyTab + CalculationsTab + MethodologyTab are wrapped in React.memo.
architectureDecisions.rationale: Calculator shells re-render on every input change. Without memo, these heavy tabs re-render too even when their props haven't changed by reference. With memo, they skip the work when the parent re-render didn't affect their props.
architectureDecisions.tradeoff: Slightly more verbose component definitions (renamed to *Inner; exported via memo wrapper). Standard pattern, low overhead.

architectureDecisions.title: DHW CalculationsTab intentionally not split
architectureDecisions.decision: DHW CalculationsTab.tsx is ~1400 lines but is intentionally kept as one file rather than split per step.
architectureDecisions.rationale: (1) It's lazy-loaded so doesn't impact first-paint bundle. (2) Helpers (Step/Formula/etc) are already extracted to calculations/Helpers.tsx. (3) The file represents the worked-example for ONE tab - splitting across files would scatter the math walkthrough where readers expect to follow it linearly. (4) Splitting would introduce refactor risk for marginal maintainability benefit.
architectureDecisions.tradeoff: Large file is intimidating on first encounter. Mitigated by clear numbered Step boundaries and the in-app Methodology tab's source citations.

issues.title: README.md kept current as of 2026-05-12 issue-remediation pass
issues.note: Updated to reflect multi-end-use scope, current engine versions, and the documentation-sync convention.

issues.title: localStorage as sole persistence
issues.remediation: ADR-001. Mitigated by export/import buttons + URL share links (DHW only).

resolvedIssues.title: Recharts bundle size in 290KB main chunk
resolvedIssues.resolution: Lazy-loaded EnergyTab + CalculationsTab + MethodologyTab via next/dynamic. Recharts no longer in the initial bundle - only loaded on first tab visit. Saves ~80-100KB gzipped from first-paint cost.

resolvedIssues.title: LightingCalculator inlines its Equipment/Energy/Retrofit tabs
resolvedIssues.resolution: All four tabs extracted to tabs/lighting/. LightingCalculator.tsx now ~105 lines (shell-only).

resolvedIssues.title: React.memo only on Methodology tabs
resolvedIssues.resolution: Added React.memo to EnergyTab + CalculationsTab in all 3 calculators.

resolvedIssues.title: No code splitting beyond Next route-level
resolvedIssues.resolution: next/dynamic now used for heavy tabs (Energy/Calculations/Methodology) — splits each calculator route into chunks loaded on demand.

resolvedIssues.title: Some `as` casts in DhwCalculator
resolvedIssues.resolution: Replaced with type-guard predicates (isGasTankSize, isGasTankType, isHPWHTankSize, isGasTanklessInput) at lines 79-93. applySizingRec narrows via type guards rather than casts.

resolvedIssues.title: DHW CalculationsTab is ~1400 lines
resolvedIssues.resolution: Documented as intentional (ADR-013). File is lazy-loaded (no first-paint cost), helpers already extracted to calculations/Helpers.tsx, and represents one tab's worked example — splitting would scatter the linear math walkthrough.

categories.name: Engineering pipeline tests
categories.purpose: Verify DHW pipeline produces correct outputs for all 19 system types and various input combinations

categories.name: Calibration tests
categories.purpose: Pin engines to published reference bands (ASHRAE/AHRI/NEEP/EPA/DOE field data)

categories.name: Engineering module tests
categories.purpose: Unit-test the engineering modules (Hunter curves, HPWH source modes, climate derivations, recirc heat loss)

categories.name: Persistence tests
categories.purpose: Verify URL encode/decode + saved-report storage including v1->v2 migration

gaps: No React component tests (no React Testing Library setup)

steps: Deploy out/ to gh-pages branch / GitHub Pages
deploymentPipeline.basePathHandling: next.config.ts conditionally applies basePath='/dhw-calculator' when NODE_ENV==='production'
